patch.value:
## Charter
Make workspace scale (item count x agent count) a measured, budgeted property of pm instead of an accident of the current layout. Initiative epic parented under pm-o2kc (storage owns the data-layout physics); indexed in pm-doxj alongside pm-n7rr and pm-usfg.

## Scope
- In: read-path indexing, storage fan-out, bounded-output/cursor pagination, claim+lock throughput, history/git growth policy, and the benchmark harness that gates all of it.
- Out: correctness races at small N (pm-dj98 owns those five open gaps), search relevance/providers (pm-ydp6), token-shape of individual payloads (pm-mpbb lean-output items pm-cfed/pm-g9xk/pm-vxxm — related, not children).

## Delivery order
1. pm-benchmark harness + published baseline (do first — everything else needs numbers).
2. ADR pm-bl8x accepted (index format, fan-out scheme, pagination contract, claim scaling, git growth policy).
3. Persistent read index -> 4. cursor pagination + bounded defaults -> 5. directory fan-out migration -> 6. claim/lock throughput -> 7. history/git growth automation.

## Relationship to the other initiatives
SDK-first (pm-usfg) means the index/pagination primitives land in the SDK and the CLI inherits them; simplification (pm-n7rr) shrinks the surface these guarantees must cover. Scale work must not fork CLI-only fast paths.
Scale-out: sub-second, bounded-token context at 100k-1M items with thousands of concurrent agents

Long-horizon initiative epic (third initiative beside pm-n7rr and pm-usfg) making the workspace scale axis a first-class engineering program: measurement first, then ADR-governed index/fan-out/pagination/claim-scaling work. Duplicate check 2026-07-06: searched scalability/large-workspace/sharding/pagination/index — only unrelated closed items matched; pm-dj98 owns correctness under concurrency, not throughput/scale; pm-ydp6 owns search quality, not workspace-size physics.

Benchmark harness proves: context/next/list/search/get p95 < 1s and default output < 5k tokens on a synthetic 1M-item workspace; 1000-agent parallel claim/update stress runs with zero corruption, zero history drift, bounded retry latency; all changes behind format_version gating with green migration tests; zero feature or behavior regression at small scale
pm context/next/list/search/get stay sub-second and bounded-token at 1M items; claim/mutation throughput supports thousands of parallel agents
Removes the ceiling on workspace size and fleet size: pm becomes usable as the context backbone for the largest projects, which is the stated product goal

Read paths are O(workspace) directory scans and outputs are unbounded; at the stated target scale (hundreds of thousands to millions of items, thousands of agents) every command would be slow and token-catastrophic

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Long-term planning evidence 2026-07-06/07 codex-agent: the scale-out initiative directly covers the user's 100k-1M item / thousands-agent target. Keep the delivery order measurement-first: pm-mi2x benchmark harness, ADR pm-bl8x, derived read index pm-801d, bounded cursor pagination pm-dfg0, storage fan-out, claim/lock throughput pm-lvyd, history/git growth policy, and then federation story pm-is1a. Implementation should land in SDK primitives first so list/search/context/next/get and CLI/MCP inherit the same bounded-output and concurrency guarantees.
